citations.desc: Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries.
citations.narrative:
At the time of inspection, the licensee was not able to locate multiple required documents, including the program of
veterinary care, the contingency plan and record of animals on hand.
Per this section, all documents that are required to be kept under the Animal Welfare Act (AWA) should be available at
the time of an inspection so the required documents can be reviewed to determine compliance with the provisions of the
AWA. Failure to provide documents and records prevents APHIS officials from verifying compliance with the provisions of
the AWA. Copies of all documents and records required under the AWA must be on the premises and available for
review. Correct by May 15, 2026.

The enclosure housing four dromedary camels contained five metal panel gates that were in disrepair. The gates were
rusted, bent, and broken in multiple areas, with jagged edges protruding into the enclosure. A pile of old sheet metal with
sharp, exposed edges was also found in the enclosure.
Failure to adequately maintain primary enclosures places animals at significant risk. Structural defects or poor upkeep can
result in preventable injuries, opportunities for escape, or even death of the animals housed within.
The facility must be constructed of such material and of such strength as appropriate for the animals involved. The indoor
and outdoor housing facilities shall be structurally sound and shall be maintained in good repair to protect the animals
from injury and to contain the animals. Correct by May 22, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.126(a)(2) 
   
Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries. 
At the time of inspection, the licensee was not able to locate multiple required documents, including the program of 
veterinary care, the contingency plan and record of animals on hand. 
 
Per this section, all documents that are required to be kept under the Animal Welfare Act (AWA) should be available at 
the time of an inspection so the required documents can be reviewed to determine compliance with the provisions of the 
AWA. Failure to provide documents and records prevents APHIS officials from verifying compliance with the provisions of 
the AWA. Copies of all documents and records required under the AWA must be on the premises and available for 
review. Correct by May 15, 2026. 
3.125(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
The enclosure housing four dromedary camels contained five metal panel gates that were in disrepair. The gates were 
rusted, bent, and broken in multiple areas, with jagged edges protruding into the enclosure. A pile of old sheet metal with 
sharp, exposed edges was also found in the enclosure. 
 
Failure to adequately maintain primary enclosures places animals at significant risk. Structural defects or poor upkeep can 
result in preventable injuries, opportunities for escape, or even death of the animals housed within. 
 
The facility must be constructed of such material and of such strength as appropriate for the animals involved. The indoor 
and outdoor housing facilities shall be structurally sound and shall be maintained in good repair to protect the animals 
from injury and to contain the animals. Correct by May 22, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
